Phase-34.NAME: MTProto Integration Stabilization Gates
Phase-34.Goal: Close the end-to-end stabilization gates for verified registration, free MTProto proxy issuance, dashboard UX, admin ops, and production edge readiness.

NonGoals.item-1: No new functional scope beyond stabilization and release readiness.
NonGoals.item-2: No deploy/install redesign outside the approved edge work.

Steps.step-1: Run full backend targeted suite for email, registration, MTProto provisioning, runtime bridge, admin ops, and migrations.
Steps.step-2: Run user frontend and admin frontend builds plus mobile viewport smoke for registration, dashboard, and admin MTProto pages.
Steps.step-3: Run security/redaction review for email tokens, tg://proxy links, derived secrets, admin audit payloads, and logs.
Steps.step-4: Run backup/restore planning update so MTProto assignments and secret rotation metadata are covered by Phase-22 artifacts.
Steps.step-5: Run production-like smoke after krotpn.xyz DNS and wildcard certificate are ready.
Steps.step-6: Prepare release handoff with known risks, rollback notes, and operator acceptance checklist.

Verification.command: cd backend && PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_email_delivery.py tests/test_verified_registration.py tests/test_mtproto_assignment_registry.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_api.py tests/test_mtproto_admin_ops.py -v

python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/modules/M-040.xml docs/modules/M-041.xml docs/modules/M-042.xml docs/modules/M-043.xml docs/modules/M-044.xml docs/modules/M-045.xml docs/modules/M-046.xml docs/modules/M-047.xml

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: Unverified registration cannot create trial, VPN, referral, or MTProto access.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Verified registration creates KrotPN trial and free indefinite Telegram MTProto proxy without duplicate side effects.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Owner dashboard, admin ops, runtime bridge, and edge smoke all have evidence.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: Release handoff clearly states remaining live-only assumptions.

Risks.risk-1: Live Telegram acceptance cannot be fully proven without production wildcard TLS and real client smoke.
Risks.risk-2: Large behavior change in registration requires staged release and rollback clarity.
